items.briefing:
Ransomware attacks are increasing in frequency and sophistication, though researchers note this is being driven by better-organised criminal groups rather than AI tools alone. Separately, security researchers have demonstrated that AI "jailbreak" techniques — methods used to bypass AI safety controls — are now being weaponised as offensive attack platforms, raising the stakes for organisations using AI tools.

On the local front, Australian finance leaders are reportedly adopting AI agents despite acknowledging gaps in oversight and governance. **Practical tip:** If your organisation uses AI tools, make sure clear policies exist around what data staff can share with them — and who is responsible if something goes wrong.

featured.title: Critical Palo Alto VPN bug now exploited by Qilin ransomware gang - BleepingComputer
featured.quote: Critical VPN vulnerability actively exploited by ransomware gang poses immediate threat to Australian enterprises.
